steps.title: Keep the sentence unified
steps.content: The sentence lists traditional design elements and modern ones, then identifies the resulting style as transitional design. The punctuation after "windows" should keep "transitional design" in the same sentence as the subject.

steps.title: Reject sentence breaks
steps.content: Choice A uses a semicolon before a phrase that is not an independent clause. Choice B creates an awkward dash break. Choice C makes "Transitional design splits" a new sentence, but the preceding sentence is left incomplete.

steps.title: Use the needed comma
steps.content: A comma after "windows" cleanly separates the introductory phrase from the main clause beginning with "transitional design." The answer is D.
